message:
beads sync: close bd-qvo19 (inet_neta shipped by peer in fa4643e0; local impl merged silently)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-qpj3","title":"syscall: renameat2 invalid flags should override null newpath EFAULT with EINVAL","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for renameat2(AT_FDCWD, ".", AT_FDCWD, NULL, 0x80000000). Add ABI regression coverage proving bad flags dominate a null destination path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:19:53.609139072Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T23:00:59.166757001Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T23:00:59.164534785Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-qq59x","title":"syscall: timer_gettime deleted timer handle with null output should still return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for timer_gettime(timer_id, NULL) after timer_delete(timer_id) succeeds, so the deleted-handle contract dominates the null-output path. Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:40:00.250837246Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:46:01.235035130Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:46:01.231817619Z","close_reason":"timer_gettime(deleted, NULL) → EINVAL pinned (deleted-handle dominates null output).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-qrq1q","title":"PORT: flopen + flopenat (libbsd open-with-advisory-lock)","description":"FreeBSD/libbsd flopen(path, flags, [mode]) atomically opens a file AND acquires an advisory file-level lock (LOCK_EX by default; LOCK_SH if O_SHLOCK is set in flags; LOCK_NB for non-blocking via O_NONBLOCK). Companion flopenat(dirfd, path, flags, [mode]) is the dirfd-relative variant. Used by daemons and daemon-managers (rc.d, supervisord, systemd unit helpers) to enforce single-instance pid files. Returns fd on success, -1 with errno on either open failure or lock failure (after closing the fd on lock failure). Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi. Implementation: thin orchestration shims over the existing open/openat + flock; handle the O_SHLOCK / O_EXLOCK / O_NONBLOCK sentinel-flag bits per BSD spec.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:48:33.404856733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:52:30.241612499Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:52:30.239542609Z","close_reason":"shipped: unistd_abi flopen+flopenat with O_SHLOCK/O_EXLOCK strip + flock-after-open + close-on-lock-fail; 8 tests; clippy-clean; rch-built","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-qvo19","title":"PORT: libresolv inet_neta (network number to text)","description":"libresolv inet_neta(src, dst, size) -> *mut c_char: format an in_addr_t (network byte order) as a CIDR-style network number string by extracting low-byte-first (which corresponds to first dotted-quad octet under network byte order). Emit each byte; skip a byte if its value is zero AND src has more non-zero bytes remaining. Insert dots between emitted octets. After all bytes, if no octets were emitted (src was 0), write "0.0.0.0". Examples: 127.0.0.0 -> "127"; 192.168.1.0 -> "192.168.1"; 192.168.1.5 -> "192.168.1.5". Sets errno=EMSGSIZE and returns NULL when dst is too small. Returns dst on success.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:13:37.311204695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:13:37.311204695Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-qvo19","title":"PORT: libresolv inet_neta (network number to text)","description":"libresolv inet_neta(src, dst, size) -> *mut c_char: format an in_addr_t (network byte order) as a CIDR-style network number string by extracting low-byte-first (which corresponds to first dotted-quad octet under network byte order). Emit each byte; skip a byte if its value is zero AND src has more non-zero bytes remaining. Insert dots between emitted octets. After all bytes, if no octets were emitted (src was 0), write "0.0.0.0". Examples: 127.0.0.0 -> "127"; 192.168.1.0 -> "192.168.1"; 192.168.1.5 -> "192.168.1.5". Sets errno=EMSGSIZE and returns NULL when dst is too small. Returns dst on success.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:13:37.311204695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:18:57.500355304Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T16:18:57.498236320Z","close_reason":"shipped: peer landed inet_neta + tests in fa4643e0 right before my commit; my local edits duplicated theirs and merged cleanly. impl + 5 abi tests covering trailing/internal/special-zero cases + EMSGSIZE paths","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-qwm","title":"CRT phase-0: _start/__libc_start_main bootstrap skeleton + acceptance fixtures","description":"Critique mapping: #4.

Deliverables:
- Implement minimal startup skeleton for controlled test binaries.
- Capture argv/envp/auxv transfer invariants and security-mode checks.

Acceptance:
- Startup fixtures execute under controlled binaries without glibc bootstrap dependence in covered path.
- Invariant ledger produced for init order and secure-mode decisions.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SwiftLark","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:10.476819415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T19:27:25.598312486Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T19:27:25.598119230Z","close_reason":"Acceptance fixture now passes under LD_PRELOAD in strict+hardened modes; verification matrix and evidence artifacts refreshed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","crt","rtld"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qwm","depends_on_id":"bd-11nb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qwm","depends_on_id":"bd-1ah8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qwm","depends_on_id":"bd-320s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-qwm","depends_on_id":"bd-3bvo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1134,"issue_id":"bd-qwm","author":"OrangeGlacier","text":"2026-02-11 initial execution increment (OrangeGlacier): audit + ownership established.

Findings:
- No current `_start` / `__libc_start_main` export exists in `crates/glibc-rs-abi`.
- `crates/glibc-rs-abi/version_scripts/libc.map` currently has no startup symbols.
- No startup-focused acceptance fixture exists in `tests/integration/`.
- `tests/conformance/verification_matrix.json` entry for `bd-qwm` is still auto-backfilled (`missing` unit/log/golden evidence).

Action taken:
- Claimed bead (`status=in_progress`, assignee=OrangeGlacier).
- Reserved initial implementation file scope for startup ABI + fixture path.
- Sent coordination updates in agent mail thread `bd-qwm` to avoid overlap.

Planned implementation increment next:
1. Add phase-0 startup ABI skeleton (`__libc_start_main`) with constrained semantics for controlled fixture binaries.
2. Add deterministic acceptance fixture (`tests/integration/fixture_startup.c`).
3. Wire symbol classification + run relevant gates; record structured artifact refs in this bead.
","created_at":"2026-02-11T19:07:57Z"},{"id":1135,"issue_id":"bd-qwm","author":"Codex","text":"Codex here. I read AGENTS.md + README.md end-to-end and did an architecture investigation (ABI→membrane→core + harness/scripts). MCP Agent Mail tool calls are currently timing out in this environment, so I will coordinate via bead comments for now. I just claimed bd-33p.1 (canonical evidence schema v2) and will push updates there; ping me here if you need anything or see conflicts.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:12:48Z"},{"id":1264,"issue_id":"bd-qwm","author":"SwiftLark","text":"Progress update (SwiftLark, 2026-03-04): started bd-qwm closure pass after bd-1ah8 unblocked this parent bead.

What I validated:
1) Startup ABI contract suite (offloaded via rch)
- Command: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test startup_abi_contract_test -- --nocapture
- Result: PASS (11/11)
- Confirms phase-0 skeleton/invariant behavior for __libc_start_main / __frankenlibc_startup_phase0 / __frankenlibc_startup_snapshot.

2) Controlled fixture evidence path
- Compiled tests/integration/fixture_startup.c into tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/raw/20260304T024556Z/fixture_startup
- Ran strict+hardened with FRANKENLIBC_STARTUP_PHASE0=1 and LD_PRELOAD=target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so (timeout 6)
- Observed outcome: both modes timed out (exit=124), captured in deterministic artifacts.

Artifacts produced/updated:
- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/trace.jsonl
- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/artifact_index.json
- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/raw/20260304T024556Z/{strict,hardened}.{stdout,stderr}.txt

Evidence registry alignment:
- Updated tests/conformance/verification_matrix.json qwm row from auto-backfilled missing -> partial:
  - unit_tests: complete
  - structured_logs: partial
  - golden_artifacts: partial
  - close_blockers: [startup_fixture_ld_preload_timeout]

Current blocker for full bd-qwm closure:
- startup fixture binary still hangs under LD_PRELOAD in strict/hardened mode (timeout). Unit-level startup ABI contract is green, but full fixture execution acceptance under preload remains unresolved.

Recommended next step:
- Isolate startup fixture hang path (likely startup interpose recursion/loader path) and land targeted fix before closing bd-qwm.","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:47:47Z"},{"id":1406,"issue_id":"bd-qwm","author":"GoldenLotus","text":"2026-04-07 closure pass (GoldenLotus): resolved the remaining acceptance blocker for bd-qwm. Root cause was twofold: (1) phase-0 startup tracing was missing several checkpoint pushes in startup_abi, and (2) the controlled C fixture was not honoring the phase-0 argv/envp contract, so the synthetic env vector was derived from an invalid stack layout under LD_PRELOAD. I also added a bootstrap-sensitive getenv fast path to avoid reentering membrane/runtime-policy orchestration while the validation pipeline is still initializing, plus a startup contract regression test that writes through stdio during phase-0 main.\n\nFiles updated:\n- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/startup_abi.rs\n- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdlib_abi.rs\n- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/membrane_state.rs\n- crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/startup_abi_contract_test.rs\n- tests/integration/fixture_startup.c\n- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/{trace.jsonl,artifact_index.json}\n- tests/conformance/verification_matrix.json\n\nVerification:\n- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check -p frankenlibc-abi -> PASS\n- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets -> PASS\n- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings -> PASS\n- rch exec -- cargo build -p frankenlibc-abi --release -> PASS\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test startup_abi_contract_test -- --nocapture -> PASS (12/12)\n- cc -std=c11 -O2 tests/integration/fixture_startup.c -o tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/raw/20260407T192033Z/fixture_startup -> PASS\n- timeout 6 env FRANKENLIBC_MODE=strict FRANKENLIBC_STARTUP_PHASE0=1 LD_PRELOAD=target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/raw/20260407T192033Z/fixture_startup -> PASS\n- timeout 6 env FRANKENLIBC_MODE=hardened FRANKENLIBC_STARTUP_PHASE0=1 LD_PRELOAD=target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/raw/20260407T192033Z/fixture_startup -> PASS\n\nEvidence refreshed:\n- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/raw/20260407T192033Z/*\n- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/trace.jsonl\n- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-qwm/artifact_index.json\n- tests/conformance/verification_matrix.json","created_at":"2026-04-07T19:27:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-r24d6","title":"PORT: strvisx + strnvisx + strunvisx + strnunvisx (NetBSD vis(3) extended-length variants)","description":"NetBSD vis(3) extended-length variants: strvisx(dst, src, srclen, flags) and strnvisx(dst, dlen, src, srclen, flags) encode `srclen` bytes of `src` into `dst`, allowing input that contains embedded NUL bytes (unlike NUL-terminated strvis/strnvis). strunvisx(dst, src, flags) and strnunvisx(dst, dlen, src, flags) are the inverse decoders that accept a flags parameter for libutil API compatibility (current implementation ignores flags since our decoder is mode-agnostic). Companion to the strvis/strnvis/strunvis/strnunvis quartet (bd-i18zl) and the vis/nvis single-byte encoders (bd-7om4b). Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi. Ship: thin shims in stdio_abi.rs over the existing core::stdio::vis::{strvis_to_vec, strunvis_to_vec} primitives — strvis_to_vec already takes a byte slice and ignores NUL termination so the strvisx variants reuse it directly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:02:26.897551787Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:06:34.738999526Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:06:34.736996704Z","close_reason":"shipped: stdio_abi strvisx/strnvisx/strunvisx/strnunvisx over existing core::stdio::vis primitives; 10 tests; clippy-clean; rch-built","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-r25ks","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] abort() does not unblock SIGABRT before raising — POSIX violation, suppresses core dump","description":"In crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdlib_abi.rs at lines 2297-2309, abort() raises SIGABRT via crate::signal_abi::raise without first unblocking SIGABRT.

POSIX 2008 mandates: 'If the SIGABRT signal is being held or ignored, abort() shall first unblock or unignore the SIGABRT signal.'

Current implementation:
1. fflush stdio
2. raise(SIGABRT)  -- if blocked, signal stays pending, no delivery, no termination
3. signal(SIGABRT, SIG_DFL)
4. raise(SIGABRT)  -- still blocked, still no delivery
5. sys_exit_group(134)  -- fallback, but no core dump

Consequences:
- Process exits with status 134, but no core file is generated (production debugging is impaired)
- POSIX-compliant programs that block SIGABRT around critical sections expect abort() to override the block; they will instead silently continue execution (because raise returns without delivery) -- but here we have sys_exit_group as fallback so termination is preserved
- The user's SIG_DFL re-raise behavior is bypassed when SIGABRT is blocked

Fix: Before the first raise(), call sigprocmask(SIG_UNBLOCK, ...) with a sigset containing only SIGABRT. Match glibc abort.c behavior: also clear SIG_IGN by resetting to SIG_DFL if currently SIG_IGN.

Severity: HIGH -- POSIX non-compliance + lost coredumps in production crashes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T01:54:15.710736731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T02:36:11.148658121Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T02:36:11.146362986Z","close_reason":"abort() unblocks SIGABRT before raise; POSIX-compliant; commit 64fb99b2","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
